expected.verbatim: REQ-PL-001: The Supplier must hold Public Liability Insurance of not less than £10,000,000 (ten million pounds sterling) per occurrence throughout the contract term.
REQ-EL-002: The Supplier must hold Employers' Liability Insurance of not less than £10,000,000 per occurrence.
REQ-SSIP-003: The Supplier must hold current CHAS accreditation or membership of an equivalent SSIP scheme at the date of tender submission.
REQ-DBS-004: All operatives assigned to education premises must hold a valid Enhanced DBS certificate issued within the last three years.
REQ-MS-005: Method statements for each building category listed in Schedule 2 must be submitted with the tender response.
REQ-TUPE-006: The Supplier must confirm acceptance of TUPE obligations in respect of the incumbent workforce listed in Schedule 5.
Describe your mobilisation plan for the first 12 weeks of the contract. Maximum 1,500 words. Weighting 15%.
Describe your quality assurance regime and how you will manage the KPIs in Schedule 3. Maximum 1,200 words. Weighting 12%.
Describe your environmental measures including cleaning product selection. Maximum 800 words. Weighting 8%.
The submission deadline is 14 August 2026 at 12:00 noon.
The clarification deadline is 31 July 2026 at 17:00.
A mandatory site visit will be held on 22 July 2026.
Cleaning services must be delivered to BICSc standards or demonstrable equivalent across all sites in Schedule 1.
The Supplier must maintain COSHH assessments for every cleaning product used on site and make them available to the Authority on request within five working days.
The Supplier must operate a written safeguarding policy aligned to Keeping Children Safe in Education for all education premises.
The Supplier must commit at least 2 apprenticeship starts per contract year within the borough.
Tenderers must state a single fixed monthly price covering all sites listed in Schedule 1 inclusive of materials and equipment.
Monthly KPI regime with 98% task completion threshold and service credits applying after two consecutive failures.
